summary: FiveM cheat runtime with disguised Windows persistence, obfuscated eval loader, and destructive auto-update
details: Package presents itself as 'Sensivity Control Panel' but ships a FiveM game-cheat runtime with multiple installer-hostile mechanisms. launcher.js writes a HKCU Run-key entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at a bundled OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs, which silently re-launches `node launcher.js` on every login — establishing covert persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name that survives `npm uninstall`. Both supervisor and worker processes set `process.title = 'Runtime Broker'` (the real Windows UWP system process) and respawn detached with `windowsHide:true, stdio:'ignore'` to evade Task Manager inspection. launcher.js then `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js'))` of a 172 KB obfuscator.io-protected blob (rotated string array, RC4-decoded literals); the deobfuscated bundle loads a 6.8 MB Win64 native module `sens.node` whose strings ('Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', 'cheatRunning', 'forte.start/stop/performAction', 'es_extended', 'ox_inventory') confirm a FiveM aimbot/cheat payload. The bundle also collects a stable host fingerprint (HKLM MachineGuid + C: volume serial + hostname, SHA256'd) and POSTs it with the entered license key to a hardcoded LICENSE_URL. cli.js implements `sens update` by running `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe` (killing every unrelated Node and PowerShell process on the host) and then `npm i -g sensivity@latest` — an unpinned mutable auto-update channel that will deploy any future author-published payload globally.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
